is there some TUI to configure the models that we use for tenex-edge?

No, there isn't. `providers.json`/`llms.json` (which drive model selection per role, e.g. `edge-distillation`) live under `~/.tenex-edge` and are read-only from tenex-edge's side — you'd edit them by hand. There's no `tenex-edge` subcommand or TUI to manage them; the only TUI in the codebase (`tmux_cli/tui_*.rs`) is the session-spawner picker, unrelated to model config.

If you want, I can sketch a small `tenex-edge models` TUI/subcommand for editing roles/configurations — is that something you'd like scoped as a task, or were you just checking if one already existed?

use inquirer crate -- make it called `tenex-edge config` -- one of the menu options is configuring providers, another is configuring models (with model listing from the different providers (i.e. list the models from the configured ollama endpoint (somtimes localhost, could be anything, could be sometimes ollama cloud, etc) -- or the openrouter models -- with fuzzy search, etc a REAL good UX while you start working on the basics launch a fable agent to give you the UX/UI (including colors, etc) so we don't make something shitty

Everything is wired up and working correctly. Here's a summary of what's built:

## `tenex-edge config` — done and verified working

New module at `src/cli/config/` (8 files, ~1000 LOC, each well under the 300-line soft limit):

- **`store.rs`** — reads/writes `providers.json`/`llms.json` as raw JSON (preserves unknown keys — these files are shared with the wider TENEX format), with unit tests
- **`catalog.rs`** — live model fetch: Ollama `GET /api/tags`, OpenRouter `GET /api/v1/models` (context length + pricing)
- **`providers_menu.rs`** — merged list+actions Select (pick a configured provider → Edit/Test connection/Remove), "+ Add provider" filtered to unconfigured ones only, masked secrets (`sk-or-…4f2a`), dependent-role warning before removing a provider, and a "assign a role now?" bridge into Models right after adding
- **`models_menu.rs`** — role picker → provider picker (skips the prompt if only one provider is configured) → **fuzzy-searchable live model list** → confirm → write
- **`theme.rs`** — one accent (cyan, ANSI-256 `45`), success/error/muted, chosen to hold contrast on both light and dark backgrounds

I ran it against your actual local Ollama instance through a real pty — it fetched and fuzzy-searched your 11 real cloud models, wrote `edge-distillation → ollama/glm-5.2:cloud` to `llms.json`, and the "Test connection" action correctly reported "reachable — 11 models." Full 389-test suite and clippy are clean.

The Fable agent's UX spec is at `/private/tmp/claude-501/.../scratchpad/tenex-edge-config-ux-spec.md` (478 lines). I applied its highest-value calls: the color palette, the merged provider-list-with-actions pattern (vs. a separate Add/Edit/Remove menu), Test connection, correct secret masking, skip-trivial-choice, and the "no providers → offer to set one up" bridge. Not yet applied (deferred as lower-priority polish, not blocking): the confirm screen's `was: <old value>` diff line, OSC-11 dark/light auto-detection, the braille spinner (currently a plain dimmed line), and response caching for OpenRouter's 300+ model fetch within a session — happy to do any of these next if you want the full spec realized.
